sources.title: Pearson Edexcel GCSE Drama Prescribed Text Guide: An Inspector Calls
sources.rightsBasis: Official awarding-body teaching guide used for educational criticism and review; only short attributed factual excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 3; Act One opening
evidence.approvedExcerpt: A celebration is underway – Arthur Birling’s daughter, Sheila, has become engaged to Gerald Croft

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 3; Arthur’s link
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Arthur Birling sacked Eva from his mill because she led an unsuccessful strike for better pay.

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 3; Sheila’s link
evidence.approvedExcerpt: in a fit of jealousy, Sheila used her influence to have her sacked.

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 4; Act Two
evidence.approvedExcerpt: The Inspector forces Gerald Croft to reveal that he had a love affair with Daisy Renton

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 4; Mrs Birling’s link
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Mrs Birling used her influence to have the committee refuse to help Eva

evidence.locator: Pearson prescribed text guide, page 4; Eric’s link
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Eric reveals that he met Eva in a bar and kept her as his mistress.

quotation.reason: The set text remains in copyright and no licensed primary-text quotation source is available to this pipeline.

sources.title: OCR GCSE English Literature Leave Taking Teacher Guide
sources.rightsBasis: Official OCR teaching resource used as short attributed factual evidence; no primary-text quotation is retained.

evidence.approvedExcerpt: Enid loses her temper and slaps her.
Enid receives the news that her mother has died and she must send money for her funeral.
A few weeks later, Del has moved in with Mai and is staying in her estranged son’s old bedroom.
Viv gives her Enid’s money and tells Del that she has walked out of her English examination
Enid has come for a reading from Mai. She tells Mai that she is worried about Del
Mai passes her notebook to Del and explains that she does not have her own daughter to pass it to.

quotation.reason: The set text remains in copyright and no licensed primary-text quotation source is available to this pipeline.

sources.title: OCR J352 DNA Delivery Guide
sources.rightsBasis: Official OCR teaching resource; OCR permits free copying and distribution with attribution; only short attributed factual excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 7; shifting leadership
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Initially it seems John Tate is the leader

evidence.locator: OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 7; Phil’s plan
evidence.approvedExcerpt: arriving at the plot to implicate an innocent person in the killing of Adam

evidence.locator: OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 8; coerced followers
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Danny, the would-be dentist, and Brian are both used by Phil in the cover-up.

evidence.locator: OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 7; Phil and Adam
OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 7; Cathy’s ending
evidence.approvedExcerpt: Cathy emerges from the role of follower to become a leader at the end of the play.

evidence.locator: OCR Delivery Guide, printed page 7; Phil’s final appearance
evidence.approvedExcerpt: his final appearance in the play – seemingly bereft and removed from the group
